Motion To Compel Responses By Judgment Debtor To Post Judgment Discovery
Off Calendar Jan-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Motion To Compel Responses By Judgment Debtor To Post Judgment Discovery

Judgment creditor CCC&P's motion to compel responses by judgement debtor CRE to post judgment discovery is denied. This motion is limited to whether CRE waived all of its objections to the discovery and does not address whether any of those objections were validly asserted or whether CRE's should be required to provide further responses to the discovery. CRE has shown that it is entitled to relief from waiver of its objections per CCP 2030.290(a) because its two days late filed responses were in substantial compliance with the applicable code sections and its failure to serve timely responses was the result of mistake, inadvertence and excusable neglect.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to extend the stay is denied as moot. The non-Cookies parties seek to extend the stay, according to the notice of motion, "pending the Court's rulings on the Petition to Confirm Final Arbitration Award and Enter Judgment Thereon by Petitioner/Defendant Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc." By companion order today, the court confirms the arbitration award, and accordingly there is no further basis for stay.

Order submitted.
